riddle:
Here is the revised riddle:

1. Seek the page about the individual who served as acting president of the University of Texas at Austin, whose first name is shared with a notable American revolutionary, and whose last name evokes a personal connection to successfully leading a state during a crisis; what month and year mark the beginning of his service?

2. There exists an article about the figure who controls trades, signings and roster construction for professional teams — on that page, count how many languages are shown among its interlanguage links.

3. How many whole Fahrenheit units result when you convert twenty-one degrees Celsius by multiplying by 1.8 and discarding any fraction?

4. If you consult the coach famous for steering Buffalo through the early 1990s title runs, determine how many consecutive Super Bowl appearances his Bills made between 1990 and 1993.

5. The number you just uncovered — add its digits together and write down the sum; this digit-sum becomes your new value.

6. In the story of Rochester’s longtime Triple-A baseball club, perform a unit conversion: convert twenty-one metres into yards and report the integer part of the result.

7. Imagine that integer as a row of light switches; when expressed in binary, how many switches are "on" — count the one-bits to produce the next number.

8. (While reading the entry for the city's professional hockey team, note the seating capacity given for the Blue Cross Arena at the War Memorial.)

9. A quick repeat conversion on the page covering the 1997 championship series: convert twenty-one metres to yards again and take the integer part for your next figure!

10. Finally, compute (clue 5 + clue 6) % 10 and submit that single digit (0–9) as the treasure of this hunt.

metadata.theme: From campus leadership to the boardroom and the bench — tracing how institutional leadership translates from a university president to sports general managers, coaches and team owners in the upstate New York sports ecosystem.
